reviews.review_msg: I must agree with others that he is rough on calculus beginners. During class, he writes too fast and jumps forward without enough explanations. Most of the class is failing, with multiple drop outs. He is willing to help students outside of class which is great. If you know the course material, you might be fine, if you don't, you are toast!
Alright teacher.  But the TAs that mark the tests are tough markers.
Good prof, tough marker.
taught very well, but his TA marked the exam terribly. One small mistake would cost you more than half sometimes.
Nice, clear prof, but the material is very difficult (I don't think the instructor can do much in this case).
well~ he's a good prof but the course itself is sooo hard!!!!
This class is hard.
Lee = textbook..... he just said what the textbook said. well, why need the teacher....and yes.. i was in it when half the class failed his course in the summer
lol! Half of the class failed last semester. He hires horrible TAs. That means you'll never get a good grade in his class.
Good prof. Very no-nonsense...doesn't really try to connect with students, but his lectures are informative and he gives good examples. The course material itself is very difficult.
He's a really helpful prof, however the course itself is very hard, so u better study alot before the exam.
Good teaching with plenty of examples and fair exams.  However the course is challenging so study hard!
Good eplanation, but not enough examples, marking scheme fair, but exam HARD! Take the course with him if you really have to.

he's definitely a smart prof, but he "thinks" that the class is smart too, and thus he teaches at a fast pace. if u want a challenge, then he's the prof. hard exams, but then the course is hard too. if ur willing to ask him, he's willing to help.

He does not give many examples..
 Teaches too fast..
 
 extremely hard exams
the final is really HARD!!!!
He is an unfriendly professor. His explanation in the class doesn't help students. He always use "this" and "that". His marking is really unfair. 37% students have failed in this course.
very helpful and friendly, but in my opinion, exams are not very easy

Great teacher.  He has a bit of an accent, but nothing too bad.  His tests are straight from the homework he assigns.  In class, he gives tons of examples so the assignments are fairly easy.  His marking scale is kind of tough(5 A's out of 75 students)

This guy is the best math prof I've ever had! He is very helpful and nice, and the lectures actually have application in the assignments! Assignments are taken from the textbook, no trick questions. Exams are straightforward.
straight-forward, clear, organized, excellent notes, exam difficulty level generally matches homework and textbook, nothing tricky.
Super easy, pretty clear, and nice prof
fair prof, good notes, good teaching style :)
He give excellent lecture notes, MTs are OK, but final exam is quite hard
Excellent prof!  His clear examples during lecture really help in assignments.
Despite sucking at math I found this course bearable. He was a well spoken teacher and created fair tests that really outlined what he had taught in lecture. Calculus and I parted on a good note!
Very good notes. Very good lecturer. Fair exams. Very helpful. Has slight accent but totally understandable and clear. Super nice and gives great examples.
He is a very young, friendly guy. He goes over math applications step by step, easy-to-hard.. and he is not those prof who likes to trick you on their exams. He is a good prof!
he is a really good prof... my friends skipped their own math class just to listen to him because he is very clear and give so much examples that it is easy to do the homework he assigned.
organized notes with very clear course content,helpful and easy to get A
Very organized teacher with awsome notes. Notes are just like high school. He answers questions somewhat well, but sometimes tells you to read the text book as you should already know it.

Midterms and finals were way too easy, just calculations-no thought required.  Good prof. if you don't make computaional errors, but if you want to deal with usefull problems, take this course with someone else.
If you haven't taken high school calculus, don't take this class. Hard as hell. The prof is helpful and after every other step he asks for questions (rarely are any however)
He is nice. The final is much easier than midterms :D I mean, there are too many questions on the midterm, but only 19 on the final (10 of them are multiple choice questinos)
Good prof.  Very structured and organized notes.  Tends to assume knowledge.

Very structured, and relevant notes. When asked questions in class he clearly explains them. However, his course is not easy, even after doing highschool calculus. Be prepared to really understand the principles behind CalculusI or you won't do well.

very nice and willing to help; notes are organized, except he does his algebraic steps very fast, which will be fine as long as you concentrate and keep your mind sharp (sometimes hard to do at 8:30 am in the morning -_-).

good notes (altho he sometimes makes very small calculation errors). he seems afraid/intimidated by the class. it's hard to make linear algebra exciting, so make sure you keep up w/the hwk and understand the theory. not inspiring, but okay.
.......I think he is ok....I mean, he could have been worse consider that SFU is filled with professors that can't speak^^
his notes are pretty organized
e.lee made calculus more complicated than it is. he seemed to be intimidated by the class and his exam's were unrelated to the examples done in class.
E.Lee seems more suited to be a TA than a professor.  When approached in the ACW, his explanations were clear.  In class, not so much.
very nice prof
Boy does this guy suck. Bring a pillow if he teaches 8:30 calc. cuz this guy knows lots of ways to fail you.
get the heck out of that class if the guy is teaching any course....he has no idea how beginning calc students feel..he was a teacher now he's a TA again....it should stay that way
No Comments
Explanations aren't clear. Easy examples in lecture, hard questions in the exam
No Comments
explains concepts bad, hard to understand him, i didnt like his teaching style
Use easy examples on lecture, exams are tricky, doesn't explain the concept
